title: Your groceries didn't get more expensive - they got smaller
text:
That bag of chips you buy every week is quietly shrinking. Same price, same packaging, roughly ten percent less inside. It is called shrinkflation, and in the last two years it has hit almost every product on the shelf.

Companies bank on one thing: you check the price, you never check the weight. A roll of paper towels quietly drops from 60 sheets to 54. A cereal box loses a few ounces. Nobody notices, and the margin grows.

You are paying the same money for less product on every single trip. That is not inflation. That is a price hike they hoped you would never see.
highlights: paying the same money for less
